item.title: U.S. Government Directs Anthropic to Shut Down Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Models on National Security Grounds 摘要
item.description:
The US government issued an export control directive to suspend access to Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, citing national security concerns over a potential jailbreaking method. The order affects all foreign nationals, including Anthropic employees, and forces the company to disable the models for all customers. Anthropic states the vulnerabilities are minor and also discoverable by other publicly-available models without bypass. The article discusses the tension between corporate AI safety rhetoric and government intervention, questioning who should set defense policies.
标签: AI, Anthropic, export control, national security, jailbreaking, AI regulation, Fable 5, Mythos 5, government directive, AI safety

item.title: Apple’s Private Cloud Compute Is Severely Limited for Third-Party Developers 摘要
item.description:
Apple offers free access to Private Cloud Compute (PCC) for small developers in the App Store Small Business Program with fewer than 2 million first-time downloads across all apps. However, there are no paid tiers for larger developers or those who exceed the limit, and the cap applies to any app the developer has ever released, not just those using PCC. This severely limits third-party access, making PCC primarily for Apple's own Siri AI. Critics argue Apple should reconsider the policy or offer paid options.
标签: Apple, Private Cloud Compute, AI, developer, App Store, Small Business Program, pricing, cloud, privacy, Siri

item.title: Trump’s Name (Set in the Wrong Font, of Course) Has Been Removed From the Kennedy Center 摘要
item.description:
Crews removed President Donald Trump's name from the Kennedy Center building early Saturday morning, hours after a federal judge's two-week deadline expired. The judge had ruled that the board of trustees' decision to rename the performing arts venue after Trump was illegal. The removal, which began around 3 a.m., marks the end of a controversial chapter for the institution.
标签: politics, law, Kennedy Center, Trump, judicial ruling, arts, Washington DC, controversy

item.title: Pluralistic: Shareholder supremacy and the precog CEO (13 Jun 2026) 摘要
item.description:
Cory Doctorow critiques Milton Friedman's shareholder supremacy doctrine, arguing that the 'maximize shareholder value' standard is unfalsifiable because it requires a crystal ball to know if a manager truly maximized profits. He suggests this ambiguity is a feature, not a bug, serving as a universal excuse for corporate cruelty and cowardice. The post also includes links to other articles, historical retrospectives, and upcoming appearances.
标签: shareholder supremacy, Milton Friedman, fiduciary duty, corporate governance, business ethics, enshittification, capitalism, critique, economics, accountability

item.title: RSA munitions T-shirt 摘要
item.description:
In 1995, during the US export ban on strong encryption, Adam Back protested by creating a 3-line Perl implementation of RSA public key cryptography, which he used as an email signature and printed on T-shirts. The US government classified the T-shirt as a munition because it contained source code for strong encryption. The Perl code relies on the Unix 'dc' calculator for arbitrary precision arithmetic, performing fast exponentiation (m^k mod n) for RSA encryption. This represents textbook RSA, not modern refined implementations. The article briefly explains the dc commands and links to a detailed 1995 commentary.
标签: RSA, encryption, history, Perl, munitions, protest, dc, cryptography, Adam Back, export control
